text: The surgeon uses the da Vinci robotic system with three arms to perform a cholecystectomy. The fundus of the gallbladder is retracted using a Stryker Mini Alligator, while two bipolar Maryland forceps are employed for dissection. The cystic pedicle is visible, and the peritoneum has been separated, preparing to create a posterior window.

The surgeon separates the cystic duct from the cystic artery using robotic instruments, ensuring careful manipulation to avoid tenting of the common bile duct (CBD). The Calot's triangle is identified, with the cystic duct positioned medially and the CBD laterally. A window is formed, and two hem-o-lok clips are applied to the cystic duct for secure ligation.

The surgeon applies bipolar electrocautery to the cystic artery, followed by cutting the artery with scissors without using clips. The gallbladder is gradually separated from the liver bed using the da Vinci robotic system.
